item.title: Surgical management of metastatic cutaneous Crohn's disease: a case series from a tertiary centre in the United Kingdom.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Metastatic Crohn's disease (MCD) is a rare extra-intestinal manifestation (EIM) of Crohn's disease (CD), defined by granulomatous inflammation of skin non-contiguous to the gastrointestinal tract. ...

item.title: Choosing NSAID treatment in axial spondyloarthritis: factors guiding clinical decision-making.
item.description: Current opinion in rheumatology - Review - Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) are first-line therapy for axial spondyloarthritis (axSpA), improving pain, function, and disease activity. This review evaluates evidence for NSAID ef...

item.title: Communicating Risk and Safety of Inflammatory Bowel Disease Therapies.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) often require long-term treatment with advanced therapies, including biologics and small molecules. The use of these agents must be carefully balanced against the ...

item.title: Anti-Integrin αvβ6 Autoantibodies as Diagnostic and Monitoring Biomarkers for Pediatric-Onset Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis.
item.description: Hepatology research : the official journal of the Japan Society of Hepatology - Pediatric primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a rare progressive cholestatic liver disease that often presents diagnostic challenges owing to overlapping clinical and histological features with...

item.title: Recent updates in human leukocyte antigen B27-associated uveitis.
item.description: Indian journal of ophthalmology - Review - There has been progressive understanding in etiopathogenesis, clinical presentation, and effective treatment of anterior uveitis (AU) over the years. Sudden onset, symptomatic acute anterior uveiti...

item.title: Large vessel vasculopathy: An underrecognized complication in Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome.
item.description: Journal of human immunity - Autoimmune manifestations occur in 25-75% of patients with Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome (WAS), commonly including autoimmune hemolytic anemia, skin vasculitis, IgA nephropathy, arthritis, and inflammat...

item.title: A Tangled Tale: Type 2 Autoimmune Pancreatitis With a Sclerosing Twist.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Autoimmune pancreatitis (AIP) is an uncommon immune-mediated condition that can mimic pancreatic cancer and present with obstructive jaundice. Type 2 AIP is IgG4-independent and commonly associated...

item.title: Extraintestinal Manifestations of Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Focus on Kidney Complications.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), comprising Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a chronic relapsing-remitting condition characterized by systemic and intestinal inflammation and i...

item.title: Spine structural lesions in ankylosing spondylitis: comparison between radiographic and opportunistic thoraco-abdomino-pelvic computed tomography assessment.
item.description: Clinical and experimental rheumatology - The aim of this study was to evaluate the prevalence and severity of spinal structural lesions on radiographs versus opportunistic thoraco-abdomino-pelvic CT (TAP CT) in ankylosing spondylitis (AS)...

item.title: The role of a multidisciplinary approach in the early and differential diagnosis of inflammatory bowel disease-related spondyloarthritis: insights from a cross-sectional study.
item.description: Frontiers in medicine - Inflammatory bowel disease-related spondyloarthritis (IBD-SpA) commonly complicates IBD. Early diagnosis is essential; however, it is often delayed due to clinical heterogeneity and the lack of spe...
